rotator-cuff-injury.name: Rotator Cuff Injury
rotator-cuff-injury.description: An injury to the group of muscles and tendons surrounding the shoulder joint (rotator cuff), consisting of the supraspinatus, infraspinatus, teres minor, and subscapularis. May result from tearing, inflammation, or wear and tear.
symptoms: Shoulder pain, especially when lifting the arm
Weakness when raising the arm or rotating the shoulder
Increased pain at night
Clicking or popping sounds in the shoulder when moving
Reduced range of motion in the shoulder
causes: Heavy lifting or improper lifting technique
Age-related degeneration (commonly found in individuals over 40)

frozen-shoulder.name: Frozen Shoulder (Adhesive Capsulitis)
frozen-shoulder.description: A condition where the shoulder joint capsule thickens and tightens, significantly reducing shoulder mobility. It typically progresses through 3 phases: freezing (pain), frozen (stiffness), and thawing (recovery), taking 1–3 years to resolve.
symptoms: Persistent shoulder pain, especially at night
Reduced shoulder movement in all directions
Inability to raise the arm above shoulder level
Difficulty with daily activities such as dressing or combing hair

low-back-pain.name: Low Back Pain (Muscle Strain)
low-back-pain.description: Pain in the lumbar region caused by muscle or connective tissue injury. It is the most common health complaint worldwide, affecting approximately 80% of people at some point in their lives.
symptoms: Lower back pain that may radiate to the buttocks or thighs
Back muscle tightness or cramping
Difficulty moving, especially when bending or twisting
Increased pain when sitting or standing for extended periods
causes: Lifting heavy objects with poor technique

tennis-elbow.name: Tennis Elbow (Lateral Epicondylitis)
tennis-elbow.description: Inflammation or degeneration of the tendon attached to the lateral epicondyle of the humerus — the origin of muscles responsible for wrist and finger extension. Common in people who perform repetitive hand and arm movements.
symptoms: Pain on the outer side of the elbow
Weakness when gripping objects
Increased pain when shaking hands, twisting the wrist, or lifting
Pain that may radiate down the forearm
causes: Repetitive activities such as playing tennis, badminton, or golf

fibromyalgia.name: Fibromyalgia
fibromyalgia.description: A condition characterized by widespread musculoskeletal pain and soft tissue tenderness, often accompanied by fatigue, sleep disturbances, and depression. It results from abnormal pain signal processing in the central nervous system.
symptoms: Chronic widespread muscle and joint pain (lasting more than 3 months)
Easy fatigue even after rest
Memory and concentration problems (Fibro fog)
Chronic headaches
Irritable bowel syndrome

plantar-fasciitis.name: Plantar Fasciitis
plantar-fasciitis.description: Inflammation of the plantar fascia — the thick band of tissue connecting the heel bone to the toes. Pain is typically most severe with the first steps in the morning. It is the most common cause of heel pain.
symptoms: Sharp heel or inner foot pain, especially with the first steps in the morning
Increased pain after prolonged sitting followed by standing
Pain that eases after walking briefly but returns with prolonged activity
Mild swelling around the heel
causes: Prolonged standing or walking on hard surfaces
Tight calf muscles or Achilles tendon
